metadata.methodology: Scientific Analysis with Statistical Variance & Savage Commentary

savage_commentary.executive_summary: Your command collection has more red flags than a Soviet parade. 80% failure rate with token consumption that would make cryptocurrency mining look efficient.

detailed_roasts.adhd-context-switch.md: The ONLY competent command in this collection. Compact, focused, actually does what it says. This is what happens when someone follows the simplicity rules instead of treating code like a novel.
detailed_roasts.adaptive-complexity-router.md: This 'router' has 70 thinking blocks and a 23% success rate. That's not adaptive complexity - that's just complexity with abandonment issues. 4.9/5 complexity score violates every rule in CLAUDE.md. This isn't engineering, it's self-indulgent poetry.
detailed_roasts.intelligent-refactor-session.md: 533 lines to refactor code? The real refactoring needed is deleting this monstrosity. 31% success rate means it fails more often than it works. That's not intelligent - that's barely functional.
detailed_roasts.senior-developer-analysis.md: Claims to provide 'senior' analysis while violating basic complexity principles. 69 thinking blocks for analysis? A senior developer would look at this and recommend a career change to gardening.
detailed_roasts.ultrathink-interactive.md: 672 lines of 'interactive thinking' with 5963 estimated tokens. This consumes more computational resources than training the original GPT model. The only thing 'ultra' about this is the ultra-waste.

statistical_savagery.token_waste: 18,775 tokens for 5 commands averages 3,755 tokens per command. For comparison, the entire CLAUDE.md guidelines are shorter than your average command.
statistical_savagery.complexity_violation: Average complexity of 3.96/5 means 80% of commands violate the 'complexity < 5' rule. That's not a guideline violation - that's open rebellion against good practices.
statistical_savagery.success_rate: 49.8% average success rate with σ=26.1%. Your commands have worse reliability than a 1970s British car. Users have better odds at a casino.
statistical_savagery.maintenance_burden: The thinking-block-to-functionality ratio suggests these commands require more maintenance than a vintage Ferrari with emotional problems.

pattern_violations_detected: Thinking blocks used as procrastination devices
anti_patterns_identified: Factory Madness: Creating abstractions without 3+ implementations
Premature Abstraction: Building complexity for imaginary future needs
Thinking Block Abuse: 70 thinking blocks in a 'router' command
Token Explosion: Commands longer than their documentation
Success Theater: Elaborate frameworks that rarely work

immediate_actions: Delete adaptive-complexity-router.md immediately - 23% success rate is unacceptable
Refactor all commands to < 500 lines following adhd-context-switch as template
Reduce thinking blocks by 80% - they're not helping
Test actual success rates before deployment
architectural_improvements: Follow the 3-Question Rule: Can I use existing? Can I solve simply? Do I need abstraction NOW?
Implement complexity scoring with hard limits
Create simple templates based on adhd-context-switch pattern
Establish token budgets per command type
Add automated complexity validation in CI/CD
quality_gates: Complexity score must be < 3.0 for new commands
Success rate must be > 70% in testing
Token consumption capped at 2000 per execution

statistical_methods: Correlation analysis between size and complexity

primary_issues: Low success rates across complex commands
salvageable_patterns: Basic MCP integration concepts are sound
conclusion.final_verdict: This command collection represents a textbook case of how NOT to implement CLAUDE.md principles. The 80% complexity violation rate, 50% success probability, and extreme token consumption create a perfect storm of technical debt. Recommendation: Nuclear refactoring required.
